title: atriplex bonus-henricus
intro: Atriplex bonus-henricus, also known as Good King Henry, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce blood pressure, alleviate arthritis symptoms, and protect against oxidative stress. The leaves and shoots of Good King Henry can be consumed raw or cooked, and they are rich in vitamins A and C, potassium, and fiber, providing various health benefits such as improved digestion and immune function. Atriplex bonus-henricus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and it is also used in culinary dishes, particularly in European and Middle Eastern cuisine.
